title: rosa canina f. cladoleia
intro: Rosa canina f. cladoleia, also known as the Downy Dog Rose or Dwarf Wild Rose, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Rosa canina species, characterized by its small size and downy stems. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its hips, which are a rich source of vitamin C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa canina f. cladoleia include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The hips of this plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
